test.command: sh -c 'cd "/home/kratos/projects/TradingAgents-CN" && .venv-mission/bin/python -m pytest tests/services/test_watch_api_baseline.py tests/services/test_watch_digest_service.py tests/services/test_mini_auth_session_boundary.py -v && npm --prefix mini run test'

blockingIssues.description: Failed login attempts leave any previously persisted bearer session intact in `mini/lib/auth-session-boundary.js`, so a later hydrate/reload can reuse stale credentials and re-open protected digest cards after auth failure.

Duplicate digest-card selection in `mini/lib/home-controller.js` compares only a single timestamp heuristic and can choose a newer placeholder or otherwise stale row over a ready digest for the same canonical `stock_code`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that broad root `.gitignore` rules can also ignore nested Mini scaffold fixtures such as `mini/data/*`, and instruct workers to verify git tracking and use forced staging when those fixture files are intentional deliverables.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `mini-app-scaffold-and-validator` review linked root `.gitignore` rule `data/` with worker feedback that `mini/data/*` fixtures may need `git add -f` to be committed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Avoid assuming `jq` is installed in worker environments; where milestone/feature filtering examples depend on JSON extraction, document a portable Python fallback alongside `jq`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `mini-auth-session-boundary` review and worker handoff both noted that the documented startup flow had to be changed because `jq` was unavailable in this environment (`which jq` returned not found).
